project: CompViz: Dynamic Benchmark for Compositional Visual Reasoning

plan.title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
plan.description: Create isolated environment and install all required packages for scene generation, model inference, and evaluation

steps.1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.2: Install generation dependencies: pip install cairosvg pillow numpy svgwrite
steps.3: Install ML dependencies: pip install torch torchvision transformers accelerate
steps.4: Install VLM-specific packages: pip install qwen-vl-utils pillow
steps.5: Install evaluation dependencies: pip install scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn
steps.6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())'
steps.7: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data/{scenes,queries,results} models/ figures/ logs/

plan.title: Implement Core Generation Pipeline
plan.description: Build the three-stage generation pipeline: Scene Generator, Query Generator, and Answer Computer

steps.1: Implement Scene Generator (scene_generator.py): SVG-based 2D shape rendering with primitives (circle, square, triangle, star, pentagon, hexagon), colors (red, blue, green, yellow, purple, orange, cyan, magenta), sizes (small/medium/large), textures (solid/striped/dotted/checkered). Include minimum object separation constraints to prevent incoherent scenes.
steps.2: Implement Query Generator (query_generator.py): Compositional grammar with Boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT, XOR), quantifiers (∃, ∀, exactly-n, at-most-n, at-least-n), comparisons (same-as, larger-than, different-from, count-comparison), and transitive relations. Support composition depth 1-4.
steps.3: Implement Answer Computer (answer_computer.py): Symbolic execution on ground-truth scene graphs. Verify unique answers and filter vacuously true/false conditions.
steps.4: Implement difficulty controller (difficulty.py): Map difficulty levels (1-4) to object counts (3-5, 6-8, 9-12, 13-20), composition depth (1-4), distractors (0, 1-2, 3-4, 5+), and relation hops (0, 1, 2, 3+).
steps.5: Create pipeline failure mode handlers: (a) Scene incoherence: rejection sampling for layout validity with minimum separation of 20px; (b) Query ambiguity: symbolic verification ensuring unique answers; (c) Attribute binding: scene-aware query generation; (d) Quantifier scope: filter trivial truth values.
steps.6: Write generation script (generate_dataset.py) with CLI interface for configurable instance count, difficulty level, random seed, and output format.

plan.title: Generate Benchmark Datasets
plan.description: Generate all required test sets for main experiments and baselines. Fixed seeds for reproducibility.

steps.1: Generate timing validation set: 1,000 instances (250 per difficulty level) with seeds 42-45
steps.2: Generate fixed test sets for discriminative power: 2,000 instances per difficulty level × 4 levels = 8,000 instances. Seeds: 100-103
steps.3: Generate reasoning type test sets: Existential (500), Universal (500), Comparative (500), Transitive (500), Nested Quant (500). Seed: 200
steps.4: Generate transitive chain length test set: 1-hop (300), 2-hop (300), 3-hop (300), 4-hop (300). Seed: 300
steps.5: Generate nested quantifier test set: Simple ∃ (400), ∃∀ (400), ∀∃ (400), ∀∀ (400). Seed: 400
steps.6: Generate query naturalness validation set: 100 Depth-3 nested quant queries with complex phrasing. Seed: 500
steps.7: Generate simplified variants for same 100 queries. Seed: 501
steps.8: Generate 5 contamination-free test sets: 1,000 instances each with seeds 1000, 1001, 1002, 1003, 1004
steps.9: Generate CVR-style odd-one-out tasks: 1,000 instances for cross-benchmark comparison. Seed: 600
steps.10: Verify dataset integrity: check all instances have valid scenes, queries, and answers; ensure no corrupted files

plan.title: Baseline 1: Simple Heuristic Baseline (Random + Majority)
plan.description: Implement simple baselines to establish floor performance. Required: at least one simple baseline.

steps.1: Implement Random Baseline: Randomly select yes/no for binary questions, random attribute for multi-choice
steps.2: Implement Majority Class Baseline: Always predict majority class from training distribution (50% yes/no for balanced, adjusted for imbalanced)
steps.3: Evaluate both baselines on all fixed test sets (8,000 instances)
steps.4: Record metrics: accuracy per difficulty level, per reasoning type
steps.5: Save results to results/baseline_simple.json

plan.title: Baseline 2: Strong Baseline - CLEVR-Style Symbolic Executor
plan.description: Implement a symbolic executor similar to CLEVR's as a strong baseline. This establishes performance ceiling for symbolic methods.

steps.1: Implement scene parser: Extract scene graph from SVG ground truth (cheating baseline - uses perfect perception)
steps.2: Implement query executor: Execute logical programs on scene graph for existential, universal, and comparative queries
steps.3: Implement transitive relation executor: Use graph traversal for multi-hop relations
steps.4: Test on all fixed test sets (8,000 instances)
steps.5: Record metrics: accuracy per difficulty level, per reasoning type
steps.6: Save results to results/baseline_symbolic.json
steps.7: Note: This baseline uses ground-truth scene graphs, so represents upper bound for perfect perception + symbolic reasoning

plan.title: Speed Validation Experiment (RQ1)
plan.description: Validate <100ms generation time target across difficulty levels. Address feedback: timing is TARGET not pre-validated measurement.

steps.1: Load timing validation set (1,000 instances, 250 per difficulty level)
steps.2: Run generation 5 times per instance with warm-start (first run may have initialization overhead)
steps.3: Measure per-component timing: Scene Generation, Query Generation, Answer Computation
steps.4: Calculate statistics: mean, std, min, max, P50, P95, P99 per difficulty level
steps.5: Generate timing breakdown report: total time and per-component times
steps.6: Create visualization: boxplot of generation time by difficulty level
steps.7: Validate hypothesis H1: Mean <100ms with σ<20ms across all levels
steps.8: If timing exceeds target: document bottlenecks and optimization opportunities
steps.9: Save results to results/speed_validation.json and figures/speed_breakdown.png

plan.title: Discriminative Power Experiment (RQ2)
plan.description: Evaluate models across difficulty levels to demonstrate >30% accuracy gap.

steps.1: Select models for local evaluation: Qwen2-VL-7B, InternVL2-4B (fit on A6000, strong recent performers)
steps.2: Load fixed test sets: 2,000 instances per difficulty level (8,000 total)
steps.3: For each model and each difficulty level (1-4):
steps.4: - Run inference with batch_size=1 (VLM constraint)
steps.5: - Use prompt template: 'Answer the following question with a single word (yes/no) or number. {question}'
steps.6: - Extract yes/no/count answers using regex patterns
steps.8: Run 3 random seeds for model inference (different generation temperatures: 0.0, 0.1, 0.2) to measure variance
steps.9: Calculate accuracy gap between Level 1 and Level 4
steps.10: Validate hypothesis H2: >30% accuracy gap between easiest and hardest difficulty
steps.11: Create visualization: accuracy by difficulty level with error bars across seeds
steps.12: Save results to results/discriminative_power.json and figures/accuracy_by_difficulty.png

plan.title: Nested Quantification Analysis (RQ3)
plan.description: Analyze model performance on nested quantifiers (∃∀, ∀∃) vs simple existential (∃).

steps.1: Load nested quantifier test set: Simple ∃ (400), ∃∀ (400), ∀∃ (400), ∀∀ (400)
steps.2: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B and InternVL2-4B on all 4 quantifier types
steps.3: Run with 3 random seeds (temperatures: 0.0, 0.1, 0.2)
steps.4: Calculate accuracy per quantifier type
steps.5: Compute accuracy drop: ∃∀ vs ∃, ∀∃ vs ∃
steps.6: Validate hypothesis H3: >25% accuracy drop on nested quantifiers
steps.7: Create confusion matrix: quantifier type vs predicted answer type
steps.8: Analyze error patterns: scope errors, quantifier flipping
steps.9: Save results to results/nested_quantification.json and figures/quantifier_performance.png

plan.title: Transitive Relation Scaling (RQ4)
plan.description: Measure accuracy degradation with increasing relation chain length.

steps.1: Load transitive chain test set: 1-hop (300), 2-hop (300), 3-hop (300), 4-hop (300)
steps.2: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B and InternVL2-4B on all chain lengths
steps.3: Run with 3 random seeds (temperatures: 0.0, 0.1, 0.2)
steps.4: Calculate accuracy per chain length
steps.5: Fit decay curve: Accuracy = 1/(1 + k × hops), estimate k
steps.6: Validate hypothesis H4: Accuracy ∝ 1/(1+0.3×hops) relationship
steps.7: Create visualization: accuracy vs chain length with fitted curve
steps.8: Save results to results/transitive_scaling.json and figures/transitive_accuracy.png

plan.title: Query Naturalness Validation (RQ4.5)
plan.description: Address feedback: validate that nested quantification linguistic complexity does not confound reasoning difficulty.

steps.1: Load query naturalness validation set: 100 Depth-3 queries with complex phrasing
steps.2: Load simplified variants of same 100 queries
steps.3: Example complex: 'Is there a shape larger than ALL red circles?'
steps.4: Example simplified: 'Find a shape. Is it larger than every red circle?'
steps.5: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B on both formulations with 3 random seeds
steps.6: Calculate performance gap: accuracy_complex - accuracy_simplified
steps.7: Measure syntactic complexity: query length, embedding depth, quantifier nesting count
steps.8: Validate hypothesis H5: <10% accuracy difference between formulations
steps.9: If gap >20%, document as linguistic complexity confound requiring mitigation
steps.10: Save results to results/query_naturalness.json and figures/naturalness_validation.png

plan.title: Contamination-Free Evaluation (RQ5)
plan.description: Demonstrate practical contamination-free evaluation with 5 independent runs.

steps.1: Generate 5 fresh test sets (1,000 instances each) with seeds 1000-1004
steps.2: For each run, generate scenes and queries from scratch
steps.3: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B on all 5 fresh test sets
steps.4: Calculate mean accuracy and standard deviation across 5 runs
steps.5: Calculate coefficient of variation (CV = std/mean)
steps.6: Validate hypothesis H6: Standard deviation <3% across runs
steps.7: Compare to fixed test set performance (from discriminative experiment)
steps.8: Create visualization: accuracy per run with error bars
steps.9: Save results to results/contamination_free.json and figures/per_run_variance.png

plan.title: CVR Comparison Experiment
plan.description: Implement CVR-style tasks for cross-benchmark comparison.

steps.1: Implement odd-one-out task generator following CVR paper specification
steps.2: Generate 1,000 CVR-style instances with CompViz pipeline
steps.3: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B and InternVL2-4B on CVR tasks
steps.4: Compare performance: CVR tasks vs CompViz tasks at same difficulty
steps.5: Calculate correlation between CVR accuracy and CompViz accuracy per model
steps.6: Identify incremental signal: cases where models succeed on CVR but fail on CompViz

plan.title: API Model Evaluation (Limited Scale)
plan.description: Evaluate select API models on subset for comparison (budget-conscious).

steps.1: Select API models: GPT-4o-mini (cost-effective), Claude 3.5 Haiku
steps.2: Select representative subset: 500 instances per difficulty level (2,000 total)
steps.3: Implement API inference with rate limiting (respect 60 RPM limit)
steps.4: Evaluate both API models on subset
steps.5: Compare API vs open-source model performance
steps.6: Estimate cost per 1,000 instances for budget planning

plan.title: Failure Mode Analysis (RQ6)
plan.description: Analyze failure modes and differentiate from MM-CondChain and DynaMath.

steps.1: Collect 100 failure examples from Qwen2-VL-7B predictions
steps.2: Categorize error types: (a) Quantifier scope errors, (b) Relation chain errors, (c) Counting errors, (d) Attribute binding errors
steps.3: Compare to expected MM-CondChain errors (conditional branching, path prediction)
steps.4: Compare to expected DynaMath errors (calculation, formula misapplication)
steps.5: Document unique failure patterns for CompViz
steps.6: Create error type distribution chart
steps.7: Validate hypothesis H7: Error patterns distinct from MM-CondChain and DynaMath
steps.8: Save results to results/failure_analysis.json and figures/error_distribution.png

plan.title: Ablation: Scene Complexity Impact
plan.description: Isolate impact of scene complexity (object count) from reasoning complexity.

steps.1: Generate ablation test sets: Fixed reasoning depth (Depth 2), varying object count (3-5, 6-8, 9-12, 13-20)
steps.2: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B on all sets
steps.3: Measure accuracy vs object count
steps.4: Determine if performance drop is due to scene clutter or reasoning difficulty
steps.5: Report scene complexity vs reasoning complexity contributions
steps.6: Save results to results/ablation_scene_complexity.json

plan.title: Ablation: Query Complexity Impact
plan.description: Isolate impact of query complexity from visual complexity.

steps.1: Generate ablation test sets: Fixed scene (5 objects), varying composition depth (1-4)
steps.2: Evaluate Qwen2-VL-7B on all sets
steps.3: Measure accuracy vs composition depth
steps.4: Compare to full complexity results
steps.5: Report isolated query complexity impact
steps.6: Save results to results/ablation_query_complexity.json

plan.title: Curriculum Learning Feasibility (Reduced Scope)
plan.description: Address feedback: reduced scope demonstration of curriculum learning capability (generation only, no fine-tuning).

steps.1: Generate Level 1 training data: 10,000 instances
steps.2: Generate Level 4 training data: 10,000 instances
steps.3: Generate Level 4 validation data: 1,000 instances
steps.4: Measure total generation time
steps.5: Calculate generation throughput (instances/second)
steps.6: Validate that 20K instances generate in <15 minutes
steps.7: Document: This demonstrates the ENABLING capability for curriculum learning, not full training
steps.8: Note in paper: Full LoRA fine-tuning experiments deferred to future work with extended compute

plan.title: Statistical Significance Testing
plan.description: Perform rigorous statistical tests on all experimental results.

steps.1: Load all experimental results
steps.2: For discriminative power: perform ANOVA across difficulty levels, report F-statistic and p-value
steps.3: For nested quantification: perform paired t-test between ∃ and ∃∀, report t-statistic and p-value
steps.4: For transitive scaling: perform correlation test between chain length and accuracy, report Pearson r
steps.5: For contamination-free: calculate confidence interval for mean accuracy across 5 runs
steps.6: For query naturalness: perform McNemar's test comparing complex vs simplified formulations
steps.7: Apply Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons
steps.8: Save statistical test results to results/statistical_tests.json

plan.title: Success Criteria Assessment
plan.description: Evaluate all hypotheses against experimental results.

steps.1: H1 (Speed): Check mean <100ms with σ<20ms [PASS/FAIL]
steps.2: H2 (Discrimination): Check >30% accuracy gap Level 1 vs Level 4 [PASS/FAIL]
steps.3: H3 (Nested Quant): Check >25% accuracy drop ∃∀ vs ∃ [PASS/FAIL]
steps.4: H4 (Transitive): Check accuracy ∝ 1/(1+0.3×hops) relationship [PASS/FAIL]
steps.5: H5 (Query Naturalness): Check <10% complex vs simplified gap [PASS/FAIL]
steps.6: H6 (Contamination-Free): Check <3% variance across 5 runs [PASS/FAIL]
steps.7: H7 (Distinct Failures): Check error patterns distinct from related work [PASS/FAIL]
steps.8: Generate summary table: Hypothesis, Target, Actual, Status
steps.9: Document partial successes and failure modes

plan.title: Generate Paper Figures
plan.description: Create publication-quality figures for the research paper.

steps.1: Figure 1: Generation pipeline diagram (Scene → Query → Answer)
steps.2: Figure 2: Speed comparison table - CompViz vs MM-CondChain vs MentisOculi vs DynaMath vs InfiniBench
steps.3: Figure 3: Accuracy vs Difficulty Level (4 lines: Qwen2-VL, InternVL, Symbolic Baseline, Random Baseline)
steps.4: Figure 4: Nested Quantifier Performance (bar chart: ∃, ∃∀, ∀∃, ∀∀)
steps.5: Figure 5: Transitive Chain Accuracy (line plot with fitted curve)
steps.6: Figure 6: Per-Run Variance (error bars across 5 fresh test sets)
steps.7: Figure 7: Error Type Distribution (pie chart comparing CompViz, MM-CondChain expected, DynaMath expected)
steps.8: Figure 8: Example scenes and queries (visual examples from each difficulty level)
steps.9: Save all figures to figures/ directory in high-resolution PNG and PDF formats
steps.10: Ensure all figures have clear captions, axis labels, and legends

plan.title: Generate Paper Tables
plan.description: Create publication-quality tables summarizing results.

steps.1: Table 1: Benchmark comparison (CompViz vs related work with attributes: Domain, Reasoning Type, Speed, Difficulty Control)
steps.2: Table 2: Generation timing breakdown by component and difficulty level
steps.3: Table 3: Model performance across difficulty levels (main results)
steps.4: Table 4: Nested quantifier performance breakdown
steps.5: Table 5: Success criteria assessment summary
steps.6: Table 6: Ablation study results
steps.7: Save tables as LaTeX source files in figures/tables.tex

plan.title: Final Report Generation
plan.description: Compile comprehensive experiment report with all results.

steps.1: Generate summary report: results/EXPERIMENT_REPORT.md
steps.2: Include: Experimental setup, All results, Statistical tests, Success criteria assessment
steps.3: Document any deviations from plan and justifications
steps.4: Report negative results honestly
steps.5: Include runtime breakdown: actual vs estimated
steps.6: Document computational cost: GPU hours, API costs
steps.7: Save all raw results in machine-readable format (JSON)

execution_notes.parallelization: Speed validation, data generation, and baseline experiments can run in parallel with main experiments if using CPU for generation. Model inference should use GPU sequentially due to single A6000.
execution_notes.checkpointing: Save intermediate results after each experiment to enable resume on interruption.
execution_notes.reproducibility: All random seeds are fixed. Document any environment-specific dependencies.
feedback_addressed: Differentiation from DynaMath: Added explicit comparison experiment and documentation of distinct reasoning focus (logical vs mathematical)
Timing validation clarification: Explicitly state timing is TARGET to be validated, not pre-validated measurement
Query naturalness validation: Added RQ4.5 experiment comparing complex vs simplified formulations
Reduced curriculum learning scope: Changed to feasibility demonstration (generation only) instead of full LoRA fine-tuning
Generation pipeline failure modes: Added explicit handlers and documentation for all identified failure modes

risk_mitigation.timing_target_not_met: If generation exceeds 200ms, document actual timing and pivot to emphasize accuracy/discrimination results. The benchmark still provides novel reasoning challenges even if speed target is missed.
risk_mitigation.models_too_easy: If models achieve >90% on Level 4, increase difficulty: add more objects (up to 30), deeper nesting (Depth 5), more distractors (up to 10). Document difficulty adjustment.
risk_mitigation.nested_quant_too_hard_for_humans: If query naturalness confound >20%, use progressive phrasing and document as limitation. Report parsing accuracy separately.
risk_mitigation.API_rate_limits: Implement exponential backoff. If still limiting, reduce API evaluation to 250 instances per model.
risk_mitigation.memory_issues: If OOM occurs, reduce batch size to 1, use model quantization (INT8), or evaluate smaller models only.
